name: Sesame Orange Scones
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Grease a baking sheet and set aside.
2. Combine sesame seeds and orange rind in separate small bowls.
In a large bowl, mix flour, remaining sesame seeds, and orange rind with brown sugar, baking powder, and salt.
Using a pastry blender or two knives, cut in the shortening until the mixture forms coarse crumbs.
3. Set aside 1 tablespoon of orange juice. Add the remaining orange juice to the dry ingredients and mix gently with a fork until the mixture clings together and forms a soft dough.
4. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead gently about 5-6 times. Divide the dough in half. With a lightly floured rolling pin, roll one half of the dough into an approximately 7-inch round. Cut into 4 wedges. Repeat with the remaining half of the dough.
5. Place the scones, about 1 inch apart, on the prepared baking sheet. Pierce the tops of the scones with fork tines.
6. In a small saucepan, heat the reserved orange juice and granulated sugar over medium heat until boiling. Stir in the reserved orange rind and brush over the tops of the scones. Sprinkle with the reserved sesame seeds.
7. Bake the scones for 15-18 minutes or until golden brown. Brush with additional orange glaze if desired. Serve warm.
